meta_title: Impact Windows in Weston, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Weston, FL. Protect your home in Weston Hills, Savanna & more. Free quotes, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Weston's Trusted Source for Hurricane-Rated Impact Windows — Built to Last, Designed to Protect
intro:
Weston, Florida is one of Broward County's most desirable communities — a master-planned city where tree-lined streets, top-rated schools, and stunning homes in neighborhoods like Weston Hills, Savanna, The Ridges, Windmill Ranch Estates, and Emerald Estates command average property values of $550,000 and above. But behind the beauty of this meticulously maintained city lies an undeniable reality: South Florida's hurricane season doesn't discriminate by zip code. Whether you're relaxing near Weston Town Center or settled into a quiet cul-de-sac off Bonaventure Boulevard, your home faces the same seasonal threats that have reshaped Broward County time and again.

Unlike coastal cities just miles away, Weston sits approximately 15 miles inland — and while that distance provides a modest buffer from storm surge, it does nothing to reduce the destructive power of hurricane-force winds. Weston falls within a 140 mph wind zone designation under the Florida Building Code, meaning every opening in your home — every window, every sliding glass door, every entry point — is a potential vulnerability during a named storm. For homeowners who've invested significantly in their properties, standard builder-grade windows are simply not adequate protection against the wind pressures, flying debris, and pressure differentials that accompany major hurricanes.

Impact windows represent the single most effective upgrade a Weston homeowner can make for year-round peace of mind, storm protection, and long-term value preservation. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped hundreds of Broward County families make this critical upgrade — and we understand the unique architectural styles, HOA requirements, and code compliance needs that define Weston's residential landscape. From the sprawling estates of Windmill Ranch to the Mediterranean-style villas of The Ridges, our team delivers precision installation, premium products, and the kind of attentive service that has earned us our reputation as The Kings of Service across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties.

section_why.h2: Why Weston Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing Above the Rest
section_why.body:
When you're protecting a home worth half a million dollars or more, you can't afford to gamble on inexperienced contractors or subcontracted crews who don't know Weston's specific permitting requirements, HOA guidelines, or architectural nuances. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined installation experience to every project we take on — and unlike many competitors, every single member of our installation team is a direct Premier employee. We never subcontract our work. That means you always know exactly who is in your home, who is accountable for the quality of the installation, and who stands behind every measurement, cut, and seal.

Our familiarity with Weston specifically sets us apart. We've installed impact windows in dozens of homes across neighborhoods like Savanna and Weston Hills, where two-story Mediterranean and contemporary designs require careful attention to custom sizing, grid patterns, and finish matching. We work directly with the City of Weston Building Division to manage permit applications, inspections, and code compliance on your behalf — turning what can be a complicated bureaucratic process into a seamless, stress-free experience for homeowners. You focus on your family; we handle the paperwork.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and has completed more than 500 homes across South Florida. Our reputation isn't built on advertising — it's built on referrals from satisfied neighbors who trusted us with their most valuable asset. We carry full licensing and insurance, and we stand behind our work with manufacturer-backed product warranties and workmanship guarantees that protect your investment long after installation day.

Finally, we understand that even high-income homeowners appreciate financial flexibility. That's why we offer multiple financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down, making it easier than ever to protect your Weston home without disrupting your household budget. From your first consultation to your final inspection, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing delivers the kind of white-glove service your home — and your family — deserves.

section_history.h2: Weston's Storm History: Why 'It Missed Us Last Time' Is Never a Strategy
section_history.body:
Weston and the surrounding western Broward County corridor have been touched by no fewer than eight named storms since 1990 — a sobering statistic for a city that many assume is protected simply by virtue of its inland location. The most defining storm in modern South Florida history, Hurricane Andrew in August 1992, decimated communities just south of Weston in Miami-Dade County and fundamentally changed how Florida approaches building codes and construction standards. Homes built before 1994 — which account for approximately 15% of Weston's housing stock — were constructed before post-Andrew code reforms took effect, making them especially vulnerable to wind and debris penetration.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 served as a jarring reminder that inland Broward County is not immune to serious storm impacts. Wilma tracked across South Florida from southwest to northeast at unusual speed, producing sustained winds that stripped roofs, shattered conventional windows, and left hundreds of thousands of Broward residents without power for weeks. Weston homeowners who had not yet upgraded to impact-rated products faced significant structural damage and costly repairs. Then came Hurricane Irma in September 2017, which brought widespread wind damage across Broward County and reinforced that the question for Weston homeowners is never if another major storm will arrive — it's when.

Each of these storms tells the same story: wind-borne debris is the primary driver of structural failure during hurricanes. When a window fails under pressure, it creates an opening that can allow catastrophic internal pressure buildup, leading to roof failure and total structural compromise. Impact windows are specifically engineered to prevent this chain reaction — maintaining the envelope of your home even when debris strikes at high velocity. For Weston families, installing impact windows isn't a luxury upgrade; it's the most responsible decision a homeowner can make.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Compliance for Weston: What Every Homeowner Needs to Know
section_code.body:
Weston falls within Broward County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code (FBC), and while the city is not classified as part of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — that designation applies primarily to Miami-Dade and parts of Broward closer to the coast — Weston homeowners are still required by law to install hurricane-rated products whenever windows or doors are replaced. This is a critical distinction many homeowners overlook: you don't need to be in the HVHZ for impact-rated windows to be legally required. Any window replacement in Weston's 140 mph wind zone must meet the applicable wind load and impact resistance standards outlined in the current Florida Building Code.

For Weston specifically, permitted window replacements must include products that carry appropriate Product Approval numbers from the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR). All products installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — including our ESW and CWI lines — carry valid Florida Product Approvals and are rated for the wind pressures applicable to Weston's wind zone. This matters not only for code compliance but also for insurance verification and future home sale inspections, where unpermitted or non-compliant work can create serious legal and financial complications.

Pulling permits through the City of Weston Building Division is a mandatory step for any window replacement project, and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this process entirely on your behalf. Our team prepares the permit application, submits all required product documentation, schedules inspections, and ensures that every aspect of your installation passes final approval. Homeowners who attempt to work with contractors who skip the permit process may face fines, mandatory removal of non-compliant windows, and voided insurance claims at exactly the moment they need coverage most. With Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're always protected — legally, structurally, and financially.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Premium Products Engineered for Weston's Wind Zone
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've carefully selected our product lines to offer Weston homeowners the best combination of performance, aesthetics, and long-term value. Our two primary impact window brands — ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — represent the upper tier of the impact window market, offering features that go well beyond basic hurricane protection to enhance comfort, energy efficiency, and curb appeal year-round.

ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) are engineered with multi-chamber frame systems that provide exceptional structural rigidity under high wind loads, making them particularly well-suited for the larger window openings common in Weston's upscale homes. The laminated impact glass used in ESW windows features a durable interlayer that holds the glass together even when struck by debris — preventing dangerous shattering while maintaining the integrity of your home's envelope. Low-E coating options are available to significantly reduce solar heat gain, which is especially valuable in Weston's sun-drenched climate where cooling costs can be a major household expense. ESW windows are Florida Product Approved and rated for the wind pressures applicable to Weston's 140 mph wind zone designation.

CWI impact windows offer Weston homeowners a complementary set of strengths, including a wide range of frame colors, grid configurations, and glass options that allow for precise aesthetic matching to Weston's diverse architectural styles — from the stucco-and-tile Mediterranean designs prevalent in The Ridges and Emerald Estates to the more contemporary profiles found throughout Savanna. Both ESW and CWI products are available in single-hung, double-hung, casement, sliding, awning, and picture configurations, ensuring that every window in your home can be replaced with a cohesive, code-compliant, high-performance product. Our team will walk you through every option during your in-home consultation so you can make the choice that best fits your home's character and your family's needs.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Weston — And Why the Investment Pays for Itself
section_cost.body:
For a home in Weston valued at $550,000 or more, the cost of impact window installation represents a smart, measurable investment rather than a discretionary expense. The actual project cost varies based on the number of openings, window sizes, frame finishes, and glass configurations — but most Weston homeowners with mid-to-large sized homes can expect to invest in the range that reflects both the scale of their property and the premium protection they're putting in place. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides detailed, transparent quotes based on an in-home assessment so you know exactly what you're getting and exactly what it costs — with no hidden fees, no surprise charges, and no bait-and-switch tactics.

To make the investment even more accessible, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down required. Our financing programs are designed for homeowners who want to act now rather than wait until the next storm season — because the cost of waiting can far exceed the cost of upgrading. A single broken window during a hurricane can allow water intrusion that damages flooring, drywall, cabinetry, and personal belongings to the tune of tens of thousands of dollars in repairs — far more than the cost of the windows themselves.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact windows are among the highest-value improvements a Weston homeowner can make. Real estate data consistently shows that impact windows add measurable resale value in South Florida markets, where buyers actively seek homes with full impact protection already in place. When combined with the insurance savings detailed in the next section, many Weston homeowners find that their impact window investment begins paying dividends almost immediately — making the upgrade financially sound as well as structurally essential.

section_insurance.h2: Unlock Significant Insurance Savings with Impact Windows on Your Weston Home
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially compelling reasons for Weston homeowners to install impact windows is the substantial reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums that typically follows. Florida's insurance market has been under significant stress in recent years, with carriers raising rates dramatically across Broward County — but impact windows give policyholders meaningful leverage to fight back against rising costs. By eliminating the need for separate hurricane shutters and demonstrating full opening protection, impact windows allow homeowners to qualify for wind mitigation credits that can reduce the wind portion of their insurance premium by 30% to 50% or more depending on the carrier and the scope of the installation.

The key mechanism for capturing these savings is the wind mitigation inspection — a standardized report completed by a licensed inspector that documents the hurricane-resistant features of your home. When impact windows cover all openings, the wind mitigation report reflects the highest level of opening protection, which is the most heavily weighted factor in Florida's wind mitigation credit calculations. Carriers including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and others active in the Broward County market are required by Florida law to apply these credits to your premium. Many Weston homeowners see annual savings of $1,500 to $3,000 or more — savings that accumulate year after year for the life of the windows.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team can guide you through the process of scheduling your wind mitigation inspection after installation is complete and provide all the documentation your inspector and insurance carrier will need to process your credits promptly. We've helped hundreds of Broward County homeowners navigate this process, and we know how to make sure you capture every dollar of savings you're entitled to. Between insurance discounts, energy savings from Low-E glass, and increased home value, the financial case for impact windows in Weston is as strong as the windows themselves.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in Weston if I'm 15 miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Weston is located in a 140 mph wind zone under the Florida Building Code, and distance from the coast does not reduce wind speeds during a major hurricane. Hurricane Wilma and Hurricane Irma both caused significant wind damage in western Broward County. Additionally, Florida law requires hurricane-rated products for any window replacement in Weston, regardless of proximity to the coast. Impact windows protect your home from wind pressure, flying debris, and the internal pressure buildup that can cause roof failure during a storm.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for a typical Weston home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: The cost of impact windows varies based on your home's size, number of openings, and the specific products and finishes you select. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides free, detailed in-home quotes so you'll have a precise number for your specific project. We offer financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it possible to protect your Weston home immediately without a large upfront investment. When you factor in insurance savings and energy cost reductions, many homeowners find the net out-of-pocket impact is significantly lower than the sticker price suggests.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact window installation in Weston, and how does the process work?
faqs.a: All window replacements in Weston require a permit issued by the City of Weston Building Division, along with Florida Product Approval documentation for the specific windows being installed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — we prepare and submit the application, provide all required product documentation, and schedule the required inspections. You don't have to navigate the City of Weston's building department on your own. Our team ensures your project is fully permitted, inspected, and code-compliant before we consider the job complete.

faqs.q: How long does impact window installation take for a home in Weston?
faqs.a: For most Weston homes, the actual installation is completed in one to two days depending on the number of windows and the complexity of the openings. Larger estates in neighborhoods like Windmill Ranch or The Ridges with more windows or custom sizing may require an additional day. Prior to installation, there is typically a lead time for permit approval and custom window fabrication — usually two to four weeks from signed contract to installation day. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps you informed at every stage and coordinates scheduling around your family's routine as much as possible.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance by installing impact windows in Weston?
faqs.a: Weston homeowners who install impact windows on all openings typically qualify for significant wind mitigation credits that can reduce the wind portion of their insurance premium by 30% to 50% or more. The exact savings depend on your carrier, current policy, and the scope of your installation. Many Broward County homeowners save between $1,500 and $3,000 annually — and those savings recur every year for the life of the windows. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all the documentation needed for your wind mitigation inspection so you can start capturing those savings as quickly as possible after installation.
